articles.summary: 'Aparthied Israel': UN rapporteur calls for release of Gaza flotilla activists UN special rapporteur on the occupied Palestinian territory, Francesca Albanese, condemned Israel over the interception of the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters, calling for the release of two activists. In a post on X, she wrote: “It is an abomination that Apartheid Israel, while continuing its genocide undisturbed, is allowed to patrol European waters, seize boats, kidnap people. Hostage taking is a crime." Palestinian and Global Sumud Flotilla hostages must be released now, she said.

articles.summary: Iran presents 14-point plan to US to end war, Washington yet to respond Iran has put forward a 14-point proposal to the United States aimed at ending the war, according to the semi-official Tasnim News Agency. The move follows a reported nine-point plan from Washington, with both sides outlining competing approaches to a possible settlement. Tasnim said the US proposal called for a ceasefire with a two-month timeline, while Tehran is pushing for a faster resolution. articles.title: Israeli strike kills three in south Lebanon despite ceasefire
articles.summary: Israeli strike kills three in south Lebanon despite ceasefire An Israeli air strike has killed three people in southern Lebanon, according to National News Agency. The agency said the attack targeted the town of al-Sama’iya in the Tyre district, adding to a growing toll from continued Israeli bombardment. Israeli forces have kept up air strikes and artillery fire across Lebanon despite a 10-day ceasefire that came into force on April 17, 2026, and was later extended.

Posting on X, Baghaei wrote that the US president had described the seizure of Iranian ships as “piracy” and had “brazenly boasted that ‘we act like pirates’”. articles.title: Kuwait halts crude exports in April as Strait of Hormuz blockade bites
articles.summary: Kuwait halts crude exports in April as Strait of Hormuz blockade bites Kuwait recorded zero crude oil exports in April for the first time since the end of the Gulf War, according to shipping monitor TankerTrackers.com. The data shows that while the country continued producing oil, it did not ship any crude overseas during the month as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted tanker traffic. TankerTrackers.com said Kuwait diverted output towards storage and refining instead of exports, as restrictions on the key maritime corridor choked off access to global markets. Gaza death toll rises to 72,608
articles.summary: Gaza death toll rises to 72,608 Israeli attacks in Gaza have killed at least four people and wounded 26 others in the last 24 hours, the enclave's health ministry is reporting. In a statement, the ministry added that the bodies of three people killed in earlier attacks had also been recovered. The latest figures bring the overall toll of people killed by Israel in Gaza since October 2023 to at least 72,608, with 172,445 wounded. The ministry added that, of that total, 828 people have been killed and 2,342 wounded since the ceasefire was implemented in October 2025.

articles.summary: Lebanese health officials: 2,659 killed in Israeli attacks since 2 March Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 2,659 people since it launched its fresh offensive on 2 March, the Public Health Emergency Operations Center said on Saturday. The Lebanese centre added in a statement that another 8,183 people have been wounded in the onslaught.
